TextENGNew: After school, Rena and I headed to the Shishibone Municipal Library in Okinomiya.
I'd been itching through class all day and left the instant the bell rang, but Shion was already there, browsing the stacks on the second floor.
``Shion!``
Shion turned and walked slowly toward us.
``Shii-chan, it's been a while... hasn't it?``
Rena chuckled softly after saying that.
It hadn't been long at all. It was only the day before yesterday that we had carried out our 'great undertaking' together.
Rena, too, must already feel that night as something from the distant past.
Or perhaps... Rena wanted to forget that night as quickly as possible?
...No, I was overthinking it.
``Hello, Kei-chan, Rena-san.@k@vS18/06/160600388. ...Let's not talk here -- shall we head to the lounge?``
We each bought a juice from the vending machine and settled into a worn-out sofa in the corner of the rest area.
A cafe would have worked too, but I secretly liked this -- the juice in a paper cup with ice, the pleasantly firm old sofa.
The library lounge had a lingering air that invited you to stay cheaply for a long time.
It was still early enough that there were barely any patrons in sight, let alone students.
``Shii-chan, has everything been okay since then?``
Rena crunched on a piece of ice before asking Shion the question.
``Yes, no problems at all.@k@vS18/06/160600390. Sorry for worrying you.@k@vS18/06/160600391. I wasn't sure if I should call or not.``
``So... what's the situation...?``
Shion lowered her eyes as I leaned forward.
``...It's not looking good.@k@vS18/06/160600393. ...According to intel I got through Sonozaki family connections, the police are searching hard for Teppei Houjou.``
Shion was a Sonozaki too. Through the family's reach -- from law enforcement to the underworld -- she could likely gather substantial information.
``...But... why?@k@vS18/02/160200396. Normally, they'd wait at least a week before launching a full search for a missing person, wouldn't they...?``
changeReason: NEW omits Rena's second observation (それにあの鉄平なら、一日や二日いなくたって普通なんじゃ) about Teppei being normally absent for a day or two.

TextENGNew: ...Rena was right. There was no reason for the police to be searching for him with such urgency.
A lowlife like him could turn up dead in a ditch and no one would bat an eye.
``...Normally, you'd be right.@k@vS18/06/160600395. But this... happened on the night of Watanagashi.``
At those words, Rena and I fell silent.
Did the police believe this was the curse of Oyashiro-sama? The curse of the fifth year...?
``A nearby resident witnessed Teppei Houjou riding his motorcycle toward the forest.@k@vS18/06/160600397. However, the police haven't been able to trace him beyond that point.``

``Furthermore, the loud shouting that the neighbors heard from the Houjou house on a daily basis went completely silent after that night...@k@vS18/06/160600399. ...Actually, the police had a specific reason to urgently investigate Teppei Houjou's whereabouts.``
Shion trailed off there, her words growing evasive.
A specific reason...? What on earth could it be?
``About Tomitake-san...@k@vS18/06/160600401. ...If you already know, then there's no point in me repeating it.@k@vS18/06/160600402. But if you don't...``
I had no idea what she was referring to. ...Rena looked equally puzzled, tilting her head.
When we both shook our heads at the same time, Shion drew a small breath and let the words pour out.
``...Last night, Tomitake-san... died.``
``...Shion, what do you mean by that?``
``Exactly what I said.@k@vS18/06/160600405. ...And to be precise, it wasn't just Tomitake-san.@k@vS18/06/160600406. Tomitake-san and Takano-san are both dead.``
It felt like being punched in the gut.
Two people dead on the night of Watanagashi. Including Teppei Houjou, that made three.
``Both of them were seen by witnesses at the festival that night.``
``We didn't run into them... Maybe they left early?``
``It's possible.``
``So something happened to them after the festival?``
``That would seem to be the case.``
``According to police information... both bodies were found in unusual states.@k@vS18/06/160600411. Tomitake-san -- suicide.@k@vS18/06/160600412. Takano-san -- likely homicide.``
changeReason: NEW correctly translates 変死体 as 'unusual states,' while OLD omits the 変 (abnormal/suspicious) element, just saying 'they found both their bodies.'

TextENGNew: Rena and I exchanged a look.
I couldn't imagine Tomitake-san being in a mental state that would lead to suicide. And what? Takano-san was 'likely' murdered?
...The night of Watanagashi.
According to Shion, a police car returning from festival security duty found Tomitake-san collapsed on the road between the shrine and Okinomiya.
Tomitake-san's cause of death was... clawing out his own throat... suicide.

``With his own fingernails... Scraping at his throat and slicing his blood vessels... tearing them apart... It was an unbelievably horrifying corpse...@k@vS18/06/160600414. Tomitake-san... drowned to death in his own blood...``
changeReason: NEW omits the detail about Tomitake drowning in his own blood (自分の血で溺れ死んだようになって), replacing it with just 'Unbelievable...'

TextENGNew: Tearing through the flesh of his own throat with his fingernails, ripping blood vessels apart... The pain of that far exceeded the limits of my imagination.
All I could picture was a horrifying image of blood.
``You can't be serious!@k@vS18/01/160100556. I've never heard of a suicide like that...@k@vS18/01/160100557. That's impossible!``
I raised my voice without thinking. If Shion was trying to scare us, I wanted her to drop the tasteless joke.
But Shion didn't flinch.
She simply waited for my outburst to subside, then continued.
``Do you need... to hear the explanation about Takano-san as well...?``
I apologized honestly for getting worked up and shouting.
And for doubting that Shion would make such a cruel joke.
``...Would you tell us?``
In contrast to Rena's composure, I took several deep breaths to calm myself, and only after I could finally breathe steadily again did I nod for Shion to continue.
``Takano-san's body... was discovered incinerated.``
Takano-san's corpse had apparently been found deep in the mountains of a neighboring prefecture, far from Hinamizawa.
The body had been burned wearing only underwear.
It was only identified as Takano-san through dental records during the autopsy.
``No clothing was found in the vicinity, and a kerosene can was lying nearby, so the police strongly suspect homicide.``
``...Nobody would commit suicide that way...``
``Exactly...@k@vS18/06/160600419. ...That's why this is... the curse... of Oyashiro-sama...``
I caught my breath.
I could feel dread steadily swelling from the pit of my stomach.
I could feel myself trembling.
...This was... the curse of Oyashiro-sama.
Three bodies on the night of Watanagashi...
The curse of Oyashiro-sama claimed one dead and one missing each year. But this year, three people were dead...?
``So that's why the police are frantically searching for Teppei Houjou.``
``Exactly.@k@vS18/06/160600421. ...As a suspect.``
A small wave of relief. ...Rena seemed to feel the same -- she glanced at me and gave an almost imperceptible nod.
I wasn't sure if 'fortunate' was the right word, but... for us at least, this was a convenient development.
Given his deplorable behavior, there was no reason for anyone to worry about him, yet every reason for him to be suspected.
changeReason: Old misinterprets sentence structure; Japanese means no one would worry about him but plenty of reasons to suspect him.

TextENGNew: If they were searching for him as a suspect, then we had much less to worry about.
``Then I suppose this works out.@k@vS18/02/160200402. ...Because this is the curse of Oyashiro-sama.``
Just like that.
Rena said it without changing her expression. ...But I didn't miss the slight smile that flickered at the corner of her mouth.
``If the rules of the curse are that one person dies and one goes missing every year... then this year's missing person is Teppei Houjou.@k@vS18/02/160200404. So... he'll never be found. It's fine.``
changeReason: OLD adds 'for them to believe' which softens the JPN 見つからなくて当然 (it's natural he won't be found). NEW is more direct and matches the callous tone of the original.

TextENGNew: ``I see...@k@vS18/01/160100561. We'd always assumed he'd be this year's victim... but that's not quite it.@k@vS18/01/160100562. He's the one who disappeared. The missing person.``

``Hah, well that works out perfectly then.@k@vS18/01/160100564. If his body is never found, everything's golden.@k@vS18/01/160100565. Nobody's going to come looking for a dead man who supposedly vanished.``
changeReason: OLD translates もっと深くに埋めなおしておくか (should we rebury him deeper) as abstract 'bury all of this,' losing the literal reference to physically reburying the body. NEW also diverges but differently.

TextENGNew: Relief flooded through me, and my heart grew lighter.
The situation had taken an unexpected turn, but thanks to Oyashiro-sama, it looked like everything would settle down safely.
At last, we could be freed from the horror of that night...!
``Right...@k@vS18/02/160200406. We wouldn't want the rain washing away the soil, and that hole wasn't all that deep--``
Rena murmured that with a calculating look in her eyes.
I tensed up reflexively, sensing she was thinking about something beyond my comprehension.
``It's the curse of Oyashiro-sama, so as long as the numbers of dead and missing match, there shouldn't be any problems.``
``But still...``
I wavered for a moment, unsure whether to say it, then spoke up haltingly.
But still... Could we really decide for ourselves what qualified as the curse of Oyashiro-sama?
And besides... what if we ourselves were--
``Keiichi-kun.@k@vS18/02/160200408. Are you worried that the curse of Oyashiro-sama might fall on you too?``
Rena pinpointed my anxiety exactly. ...I must have had it written all over my face again.
``Yeah, the thought crossed my mind... but no matter how I think about it, there's no reason for the curse to target us.``
``...It's because we haven't done anything wrong.@k@vS18/01/160100569. ...Because we...@k@vS18/01/160100570. fought for Satoko's sake... so there's no reason for us to be cursed.``
changeReason: NEW omits the specific reference to fighting for Satoko (沙都子のために戦った) and the logical conclusion about the curse (祟られる理由がない), replacing it with a vague 'did the right thing.'

TextENGNew: ...Or maybe I just wanted to believe that.
...I couldn't bring myself to say that part out loud.
changeReason: Old says 'impossible to be sure' but 口にできなかった means 'couldn't bring myself to say it.'

TextENGNew: ``That's right.@k@vS18/06/160600424. This is no time to be losing your nerve, Kei-chan.``
Ahahaha... Shion and I shared a forced, bright laugh.
Next to the cheerfully encouraging Shion, Rena was staring down at the floor with a dark expression.
Every year, one person dies and one disappears -- that's the curse of Oyashiro-sama...
But this year, three people had died. Did that mean... three people would disappear as well?
``...Well, we should probably get going.``
Rena stood and gave a long stretch.
I stood too... and finally voiced the thing that had been nagging at me.
``Shion, where is Satoko...?``
``Shii-chan...?``
I was about to press Shion further, but... I noticed that her face had gone rigid, staring straight ahead.
And when I followed her line of sight, there stood--
``Ah, excuse me.@k@vS18/11/160700002. Nfu fu fu...``
A heavyset, middle-aged man with a cigarette dangling from his lips was standing at the entrance to the lounge. His name was Ooishi, I think... a detective?!
``Ooishi-san...@k@vS18/06/160600427. How long have you been standing there?@k@vS18/06/160600428. Eavesdropping on law-abiding citizens isn't very admirable, you know.``
``I'd very much like for you to remain law-abiding citizens.``
Ooishi seemed to pay no mind to Shion's challenge, looking between Rena and me instead.
His loaded response left me momentarily speechless. A bad feeling gripped me, and I reflexively looked away.
``Ah, it's been a while, Maebara-san.@k@vS18/11/160700005. Let me reintroduce myself.@k@vS18/11/160700006. I'm Ooishi, from the Okinomiya Police Station.``
``Now... where's my notebook...?@k@vS18/11/160700008. Ah, here it is.``
Ooishi thrust a worn-out notebook right under our noses.
The police had come... to us? No way.
The only people who knew we'd be here today were Shion, Rena, and me.
A police officer showing up had to be pure coincidence... right?
``Now, you're Maebara-san, and... you must be Ryuugu-san... correct?``
The deliberate way Ooishi confirmed our identities grated on me. It felt like flies buzzing inside my skull.
I need to leave. I need to get away. I don't want to hear a single word from this man...
What was he here for? What did he come to ask me?
``Is there something you need from us?``
``I've been hearing quite a lot about you all.@k@vS18/11/160700011. Hinamizawa is a small village, after all.@k@vS18/11/160700012. You hear things whether you want to or not.``
changeReason: OLD changes the subject from 'I' (Ooishi talking about himself) to 'they' (the villagers), and NEW further diverges with 'you hear things.' JPN 顔と名前くらい、すぐ覚えちゃいますよ means Ooishi saying he quickly memorizes faces and names.

TextENGNew: Ooishi crushed out his cigarette and turned to face me directly.
Tension shot through my entire body.
How long had this man been here?
How much of our earlier conversation had he heard...?
What purpose had brought him here?
Sweat erupted from every pore as I sternly told myself: do not say anything careless.
``Nfu fu fu.@k@vS18/11/160700014. ...I seem to have made you all rather tense by announcing I'm police.@k@vS18/11/160700015. I didn't mean to make this feel like an interrogation.``
``No, no, I just wanted to have a little chat.@k@vS18/11/160700017. Nothing deep at all.``
Ooishi laughed heartily.
There wasn't a shred of humor in what he'd said -- the laughter only made his intention to put us at ease all the more transparent.
...As if I'd ever let something slip.
``We were actually just about to leave.@k@vS18/06/160600431. If you want to chat, another time, please.``
Shion moved to leave the lounge.
Called back at the worst possible moment, I stopped in my tracks despite myself, clicking my tongue in frustration.
So this was what they meant by a seasoned detective.
A crafty old fox...
``Well, there's just one little thing I'd like to ask.@k@vS18/11/160700021. The night before last... Maebara-san... where were you?``
My heart jolted.
Was the police already suspecting me?! For the murder of Teppei Houjou...?!
The night before last... Why didn't he say 'the day of Watanagashi'? Why limit it to the night?
Did he have some kind of evidence that led him to ask me that...?
I stole a glance at Rena beside me. Her face was deliberately blank, staring straight ahead.
``The night before last... you mean the night of the Watanagashi festival, right?@k@vS18/01/160100573. Of course, I was at the festival, having a good time...``
I chose my words with extreme care, silently praying not to let a single unnecessary word slip.
``Oho, the festival, eh?@k@vS18/11/160700023. Wonderful. I attend the Watanagashi festival every year myself.@k@vS18/11/160700024. ......Though in my case, it's for security duty.``
``Nfu fu fu.``
``......And what did you do at the festival?``
``What did I do...? I had an eating contest at one of the stalls, played the shooting gallery, things like that.``
Carefully. Carefully.
It was fine. I had genuinely attended the festival. That was an established fact.
``Around what time were you at the festival, Maebara-san?``
Here it came... If I answered the time carelessly, it would reveal that I'd arrived at the festival late.
I had to insist, right here and now, that I'd been at the festival from the very start...!
``Well, I wasn't really keeping track of the time...@k@vS18/01/160100576. It was my first time at the festival, and with all the excitement... I wasn't paying attention to the clock at all.``
``Nfu fu fu, I suppose so.@k@vS18/11/160700029. ...And how about you, Ryuugu-san?``
``Um... I was with Keiichi-kun the whole time.``
``The whole time?@k@vS18/11/160700031. Together for the entire festival?``
``Yes.@k@vS18/02/160200414. ...We were all together.``
Rena smiled pleasantly, as though reminiscing about happy memories.
All the effort we'd spent dragging our exhausted bodies through the festival -- if it wasn't going to pay off now, when would it?!
``Yeah, everyone was carrying on together...@k@vS18/01/160100578. Shion's older sister Mion absolutely crushed the eating contest, you know?``
I fell in step with Rena's tone.
``Well then... Shion Sonozaki-san.@k@vS18/11/160700033. How about you?@k@vS18/11/160700034. Were you at the festival as well?``
Rena and I went silent again.
The conversation, which had just been flowing so naturally, froze in an instant.
``......I was working my part-time job.``
``...Part-time job?``
Ooishi raised an eyebrow.
I scrambled to think of how to back up Shion's sudden excuse.
``Part-time job...?@k@vS18/11/160700037. Even though all your friends were at the festival together, you were the only one working?``
``Yes.@k@vS18/06/160600434. ...Kei-chan and I go to different schools.@k@vS18/06/160600435. We're not exactly friends.``
We're not exactly friends.
I felt a pang of discomfort at how unhesitatingly Shion said that.
And beyond the discomfort, I felt an inexplicable unease -- but I kept quiet for the time being. With Ooishi here, now was not the time to interject.
``I see...@k@vS18/11/160700039. And what kind of place do you work at?``
``A family restaurant.@k@vS18/06/160600437. It's called Angel Mort... it's fairly well-known around here.``
``Ah, I'm quite familiar with it.@k@vS18/11/160700041. ...Quite.``
``...They have really cute uniforms there.``
I'd finally found an opening to help. ...Or so I thought, until I realized after the fact that what I said was completely useless.
``Angel Mort... is it?@k@vS18/11/160700043. Was it... busy the night before last?``
``Fairly busy, I suppose...@k@vS18/06/160600439. If you want to confirm whether I was really there, ask my uncle.@k@vS18/06/160600440. He owns the place.``
``Ah, the Sonozaki family certainly runs a wide range of businesses.@k@vS18/11/160700045. Very well, I'll take you up on that.``
``So... just to be thorough, let me ask you as well, Shion-san...@k@vS18/11/160700047. On the night of Watanagashi, what hours were you working?``
I could see Shion growing more and more visibly irritated under Ooishi's relentless questioning.
``Well... I didn't get home until after midnight.@k@vS18/06/160600442. I really do need to get to my shift right now.@k@vS18/06/160600443. So if you'll excuse me.``
Shion rattled that off in a burst of frustration, then walked away at a brisk pace.
Rena and I hurried after her.
Surprisingly... Ooishi made no move to follow.
When I glanced back on my way out of the library, Ooishi was in the lounge, lighting a fresh cigarette.
In the end, Shion had stormed out and we lost sight of her.
Had she really gone to work...?
``Hey, Keiichi-kun...``
Rena looked at me anxiously as she unlocked her bike at the rack.
``Satoko-chan is safe, right...?``
``Y-yeah.@k@vS18/01/160100581. ...Shion says she's fine. So she must be fine, right?``
``Right.@k@vS18/02/160200418. ...I just had a bad feeling for some reason.@k@vS18/02/160200419. ...It's just my imagination, right?``
Rena climbed onto her bike and started pedaling hard.
Rena, too, was living through the present while carrying something unsettled in her heart.
Even after that night was behind us... even after we'd fought together to strike down a cruel, painful reality... the peace we'd known still hadn't returned.
``I want to see Satoko as soon as possible...``
Pedaling side by side, I found myself thinking about how precious it was -- those everyday moments when we could all laugh together without a care in the world.
